danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures (moles, tree trunks, birds, fish, cannons)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to Felix; collision causes magic power loss or life loss if already depleted

danger_signals.visual_form: Magic level indicator decreasing (visual representation not documented but gameplay-critical)
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability state—next hit results in death rather than power downgrade

danger_signals.visual_form: Level geometry extending past the bottom boundary of the screen
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death hazard; represents environmental danger distinct from enemies

danger_signals.visual_form: Time counter (visual form not specified in sources)
danger_signals.meaning: Resource depletion leading to life loss if timer reaches zero

danger_signals.visual_form: Eight distinct boss entity designs at end of worlds
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated threat level; represents world-state progression and danger intensity increase

safety_signals.meaning: Resource replenishment; indicates recovery opportunity from damage
Resource replenishment; restores magic level and defensive capability
Safe passage; lack of this signals danger zones

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Felix the Cat character sprite; magic wand (modified in re-release to face sideways instead of forward)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Locations Felix navigates toward (rescue mission framing)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Replaced Poindexter in 2024 re-release due to character rights; represents malevolent force
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Final destination; kidnapping of Kitty establishes him as primary opposition

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Kitty Cat character (replaced Poindexter on 2024 re-release title screen)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: End-goal location; presence/absence signals narrative state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Title screen displays The Professor as threat; Kitty absent from Felix's side
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Felix must traverse nine worlds; enemy placement and boss encounters escalate progression toward rescue

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Smaller, slower-paced enemy encounters; basic projectile-based threats (moles, tree trunks, birds)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies 'follow a regular pace back and forth'; predictable patterns; lower damage pressure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss monsters with distinct designs; more complex threat arrangements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounters require adapted attack strategy; higher lethality

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual indicator of magic level (exact form not documented); sprite changes or HUD representation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Felix's attack capability changes 'depending on the magic level'; reduced offensive effectiveness

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Original release: 'aggressively dark palette'; Re-release 2024: corrected, lighter palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No gameplay difference; visual tone communicates mood—dark palette = threatening, corrected palette = accessible

environmental_storytelling.example: Professor steals Kitty instead of magic bag (narrative shift from previous Felix games); visual absence of Kitty on title screen establishes plot motivation

Rock Bottom character modified to remove cigarette in 2024 re-release; cannon redesigned to be less realistic; tank repositioned with Felix behind glass (more 'toy-like'). These changes communicate contextual appropriateness and target audience tone
Enemy variety (moles, tree trunks, birds, fish, cannons) suggests themed world progression; aquatic enemies (fish) appear in water levels; airborne threats (birds) in sky levels (implied by variety)
Nine distinct worlds create narrative escalation; visual progression from starting point toward Professor's location

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Standard enemies (moles, tree trunks, birds, fish)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Follow regular pace back and forth; predictable movement patterns communicate low-threat routine
Full attack capability; sprite displays forward-facing or sideways orientation depending on version
Extreme vulnerability; next hit causes instant death; must seek magic replenishment items
Not specified in sources, but eight distinct designs suggest varied attack patterns and threat signatures
Fire projectiles (cannonballs); visual threat indicator; can be defeated by Felix's attacks

notes: Felix the Cat is a straightforward platformer with minimal visual-world-state complexity. The game does not feature faction systems, political situations, or complex environmental communication. Danger and safety are signaled primarily through enemy presence/absence and resource availability. The 2024 re-release represents a significant visual tone shift (dark palette → light palette) but no gameplay changes. Character substitutions (Poindexter → Kitty, Rock Bottom cigarette removal, gun redesign) reflect licensing/content appropriateness rather than narrative world-state communication. The game prioritizes mechanical clarity (jump, attack, magic levels, health) over environmental storytelling.
